campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The City Council reviewed how the city's money was invested and spent during July 2025, checking the monthly financial reports to ensure everything balanced properly. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, including different ways for people to get around the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 financial accounts and where city money was invested
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell's plan to improve transportation options and mobility for residents

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide which organizations would receive grant money for events in the upcoming fiscal year. They approved funding for specific groups, set the total amount of money available through the Competitive Event Grant Program, and made updates to the rules for how community events and street closures are handled.
keyTopics: Approved grants for specific event organizations and their individual funding amounts
Set total budget for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants to clarify requirements

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings, including live translations in over 50 languages. The meeting was available to observe through multiple channels like cable TV, YouTube, Zoom, and phone, though no public comment period was mentioned in the available agenda highlights.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple ways to watch: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, Zoom, and phone dial-in
Meeting held on March 25, 2026
No public comment period listed in agenda

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. If you were planning to attend or had zoning questions, the meeting has been rescheduled for April 8, 2026 instead.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
New date: April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development grants that help pay for housing and support services in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting notes from their previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking money to help residents with housing and human services needs.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications reviewed
Housing assistance and support services funding decisions
February 2026 meeting minutes approved
Local organizations presenting housing/human services proposals

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses in Cupertino grow and succeed. They discussed ways to reach out to shop owners about licenses and taxes, promote local shopping during the summer, partner with businesses to sponsor city events, and offer financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get current on licenses
Outreach to shopping centers and retail areas
Summer Shop Local campaign to boost local spending
Public-private partnerships for city event sponsorships
Financial incentives to attract new retail and startup businesses

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they addressed several items that were postponed from previous meetings. The council approved a plan to remove weeds around the city as part of a county-wide program, and they opened public comment on how federal housing grant money should be spent in the city for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026
Federal HUD housing grant funding decisions for FY 2026/27
Multiple agenda items continued/postponed from prior meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to honor Women's History Month and approve two agreements: one with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and another to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for three years at about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
